name: Persona
essence: Persona is Latin for 'mask'. According to Jung, it is the face you show to the world: your role at work, as a parent, your shop-window on social media. Necessary for survival, but if you identify with it fully you forget your inner self.
lightAspect: It makes social life easier, lets you strike the right tone in the right room. With a healthy persona you can do business, draw boundaries, and create a protective layer.
shadowAspect: If the mask sticks to your face, you lose touch with your own feelings. Burnout, identity crisis and the feeling of 'I don't know what I feel' all grow from here.
dreamMeaning: Dreaming of masks, costumes, uniforms, makeup or not recognising yourself means you are questioning your persona. Your unconscious is asking 'which one is the real you?' Dreams where the mask falls call you back to authenticity.
wakingMeaning: If you notice you are constantly playing a role, or you think 'I wish people really saw me', your persona is dominant. Be careful when you become a completely different person between home, work and friends: your essence must be preserved across all of them.
advice: Today wear and remove your mask consciously. When you are alone in the evening ask: 'How would I behave if no one were watching?' That answer is the authentic you.
affirmation: I play roles but I am not my roles. I know what is behind my mask.

name: Shadow
essence: The Shadow is everything you do not accept in yourself: your anger, jealousy, ambition, laziness, sometimes even rejected goodness. Jung says: 'To know your shadow is not to become enlightened, but to consciously see your darkness.'
lightAspect: Whoever meets their shadow becomes whole; creativity, courage and real strength are born from integrating the shadow. It is the source of art, humour and genuine friendship.
shadowAspect: When the shadow is repressed it gets projected outward: you hate qualities in others that exist in yourself. Group hostilities, projections and family scapegoating all begin here.
dreamMeaning: The dark figure chasing you, the cellar, a dilapidated house, a voice behind a closed door — even a threatening person of your own sex — are shadow representations. Dreams where you try to talk to it show that transformation has begun.
wakingMeaning: If you hate someone intensely and cannot explain why, that person may be the mirror of your shadow. A trait you say 'I would never have' often hides inside you. The moments that trigger you are the call of the shadow.
advice: Today think of the last time you were triggered. Which trait of the other person really disturbed you? Is a small seed of that trait in you too? Don't repress — recognise.
affirmation: My darkness belongs to me as well. Seeing it sets me free.

name: Anima
essence: Anima is the feminine soul inside the male psyche. Intuition, feeling, inspiration, lunar energy. Jung says: when a man falls in love with a woman in the outer world, most of the time he is in love with his own anima.
lightAspect: A consciously integrated anima is the source of art, compassion and deep relationships. It teaches a man to read his feelings, to cry, to create.
shadowAspect: A repressed anima becomes either melodrama (falling in love with every woman, looking for someone to save him) or misogyny ('women are too dramatic'). Sulkiness, touchy moods and projective love all flow from here.
dreamMeaning: For a man, dreaming of a mysterious woman, a water nymph, a mother, a lover or a wise old woman is the anima speaking. Whatever the figure says is a need of your soul. Chasing a lost woman points to unintegrated feeling.
wakingMeaning: If a man suddenly falls in love, starts crying or creativity explodes, his anima is awakening. If the opposite — quick anger, avoiding feelings — the anima is being suppressed. For women, the equivalent is reading 'the inner feminine wisdom' inside themselves.
advice: Today sit with a feeling without judgement. Listen to music, cry, read poetry. Surrender to rhythm rather than logic for an hour.
affirmation: I honour the feminine wisdom inside me. Feeling is not weakness.

name: Animus
essence: Animus is the masculine soul inside the female psyche. Logic, will, courage to step into the world, drawing boundaries. Jung says a healthy animus teaches a woman to say 'no' and to materialise her dreams.
lightAspect: A mature animus gives a woman clarity, resolve and independence. She speaks for herself, draws limits and owns her own authority.
shadowAspect: A repressed animus turns the inner voice into a cruel critic: 'You are inadequate, you can't do it.' Or it makes the woman dependent on authoritarian men, seeking power outside.
dreamMeaning: For a woman, dreaming of a wise old man, a soldier, a father, a lover or a crowd of men is the animus's message. If you argue with him, the process of owning inner authority has begun. The attacking male figure is the voice of the internalised critic.
wakingMeaning: When a woman in her life feels 'now my word is law', starts her own business, draws boundaries, the animus is speaking. The opposite — constantly seeking validation, mistrusting her own opinion — is the animus repressed.
advice: Today make a small decision and do not consult anyone. Find the answer inside, give it, act on it. This is the animus's workout.
affirmation: I have my own authority. The power to set direction is inside me.

name: Self
essence: The Self is the unified totality of consciousness and the unconscious — the centre and sum of the psyche. Jung called it the 'inner God-image'. It is the destination of the individuation journey, not the ego.
lightAspect: Contact with the Self gives the feeling of being 'in the right place'; religious experience, deep intuition, mandala visions, dissolution of boundaries. Inner peace and meaning come from this axis.
shadowAspect: If the ego is confused with the Self it inflates; it slips into narcissism with phrases like 'I am enlightened, I am the guide'. Spiritual bypass and messiah complex are fed here.
dreamMeaning: Mandalas, circles, squares, foursomes, crystals, glowing children, a royal couple or a star in your dreams are signs of the Self. They usually arrive after a crisis or at a turning point — the message is 'remember, you are whole'.
wakingMeaning: If you feel an indescribable wholeness, a deep peace, a sense of 'I am in the right place right now', the Self is speaking. The synchronicities crossing your path are signs of the Self.
advice: Today draw or colour a mandala. Feel the centre. Who are you at the centre of your life? Answer not from your ego but from deeper.
affirmation: I am more than the sum of my parts. My centre is always waiting for me.

name: Hero
essence: The Hero hears the call and crosses the threshold. In Campbell's monomyth the hero leaves home, is tested, faces the shadow, claims the reward and returns to share it. It is the map from adolescence to maturity.
lightAspect: The hero is courage, responsibility, growth. By doing the thing you say 'I can't do' you grow; every obstacle becomes a gift.
shadowAspect: The false hero falls into a rescuer syndrome — trying to save everyone, ignoring his own wound. Or he speaks like a hero without going on the journey ('keyboard warrior').
dreamMeaning: Holding a sword in dreams, fighting a dragon / monster / snake, climbing a mountain, finding your way through a labyrinth, are calls of the hero. Even dreams of defeat actually say 'you aren't ready yet — prepare'.
wakingMeaning: You are at a threshold in life: new job, new city, breakup, parenthood, illness. The Hero archetype activates precisely at these threshold moments. Fear is normal — the hero is not someone who isn't afraid, but someone who moves despite the fear.
advice: Today pick the smallest thing you have been saying 'no' to. Say 'yes' for three minutes. The threshold is there.
affirmation: I hear the call and I set out. The journey will recognise me when I return.

name: Wise Old Sage
essence: The Wise Old (senex) — Merlin, Gandalf, Hızır, Yoda. The inner voice that appears in the hard moments of the journey saying 'the path goes this way'. According to Jung it is the wisdom rising from the unconscious in moments of crisis.
lightAspect: Intuitive wisdom, patience, perspective. You pause in decisions, take a deep breath, know the right timing.
shadowAspect: Dry intellectual, the philosopher cut off from feeling, the manipulator who poses as a knower while actually controlling. The ego that says 'I'll speak, you do'.
dreamMeaning: If an old man, dervish, hodja, librarian, doctor or white-bearded figure tells you a sentence in a dream — write it down. That sentence is a spiritual prescription. The wise old usually arrives just before a critical decision.
wakingMeaning: If a mentor crossed your path or sudden clarity arrived on a long-held question, the wise old is at work. Books, teachers and therapists are external representatives of this archetype.
advice: Today write a letter to your 80-year-old self. 'What would you say about my biggest worry today?' Listen to the reply. That is you — the future version.
affirmation: The wise one inside me speaks. I am learning to be quiet and listen.

name: Great Mother
essence: The Great Mother archetype is not only the biological mother; she is nature, the earth, creative female energy, the nourishing womb itself. Demeter, Cybele, Mary, Mother Earth — all faces of the same archetype.
lightAspect: Unconditional love, nourishment, protection, creation. The archetype of the hand hoeing the soil, the lap embracing the child, the palm that grows plants.
shadowAspect: The devouring Mother — not nurturing but suffocating, controlling. The mother who never lets her child grow up. Our inner voice of 'I am never enough' also comes from here.
dreamMeaning: Dreams of a mother, grandmother, nursing home, breast, cave, sea or fertile soil signal a need for nourishment. The devouring mother figure is the time to leave dependence behind.
wakingMeaning: If you feel exhausted, neglected, thirsty, you need the Great Mother — not necessarily yours, but nourishment itself. The desire to control another to death is the shadow of the Great Mother.
advice: Today mother yourself: eat something warm, sleep early, hug someone. Nourishment is first an inner attitude, not an outer resource.
affirmation: I deserve to be nourished. Taking care of myself is not selfish.

name: Trickster
essence: Trickster — Hermes, Loki, Anansi, Nasreddin Hoca, Keloğlan. By breaking the rules, the trickster actually reveals a truth. The jester is wiser than the king because he can say anything.
lightAspect: Humour, creativity, cracking the status quo. In a stuck situation, a joke or an off-script move opens the door. The source of art and revolution.
shadowAspect: Cheat, manipulator, the one who breaks his word, deceives everyone, recognises no boundary. The trickster at its extreme is close to a sociopath.
dreamMeaning: Dreams of clowns, realising you've been fooled, finding a lost object, being conned, or unexpected slapstick scenes are signs of the trickster. Something in your life may not be what it seems.
wakingMeaning: When something is taken too seriously the trickster steps in — a laugh, an absurd idea, a passion-burst can change everything. Being conned or cheated is also the trickster's hard lesson.
advice: Today break a rule in a small point. Walk against traffic, try a food you don't like, smile at a stranger. Trickster creates flow.
affirmation: Every seriousness has an artifice. My playfulness opens a path.

name: Inner Child
essence: The Inner Child is your earliest self still living inside you. It carries both the divine child (pure joy, curiosity, wonder) and the wounded child (abandonment, neglect, fear) at once.
lightAspect: The divine child is the source of creativity, play and a sense of wonder. The side that can say 'wow!', that gets caught up in play, that looks at the world with awe.
shadowAspect: The wounded child throws tantrums in an adult body — jealousy attacks, abandonment panic, sabotage. The adult's crises are often this child's cries for care.
dreamMeaning: Dreams of a small child, your own childhood, the old school, your parents' house, a toy — the inner child is speaking. If the child is crying it is time to embrace that wound. If the child is laughing the joyful side is awakening.
wakingMeaning: If sudden 'unreasonable' sadness, panic of being left, disproportionate reaction to a small event — that is the inner child. At the same time, doing what you loved as a child (drawing, dancing, running) feeds it.
advice: Today look at a photo from your childhood. Say to that child 'I see you, you are safe, I am here'. Mean it, with eye contact.
affirmation: The child inside me is my responsibility. I am no longer leaving them alone.

name: Lover
essence: The Lover archetype is not only romantic love — it is the capacity to fall in love with art, an idea, a cause, nature, with God. The god Eros, the path of Mecnun, Rumi's Shams.
lightAspect: Enthusiasm, creativity, the experience of merging, courage to bond. The Lover sees the world in colour and reminds others of it.
shadowAspect: Addiction, obsession, being lost. Not being able to exist without the other, abandoning yourself, seeking romantic salvation. Adult responsibilities decay in the shadow of love.
dreamMeaning: Falling in love in a dream, reuniting, running away, seeing an ex — signs of the Lover archetype. Most often the dream-figure isn't real — the quality it represents (freedom, warmth, danger) calls to you.
wakingMeaning: If you fall in love with something/someone, the whole world lines up. The opposite — if nothing has excited you for a long time, the Lover archetype is asleep; you need to call your creativity.
advice: Today stop for a second and look at anything in front of you with 'lover's eyes': a leaf, an object, a face. Let Eros catch fire again.
affirmation: I live by loving. I do not fear to bond.

name: Creator (Artist)
essence: The Creator archetype — daimon. The compulsion to express that comes from within. If a writer doesn't write she gets sick; if a painter doesn't paint, he falls into darkness. Art is not a choice, it is a need.
lightAspect: Giving form, transforming, making meaning. Sitting opposite a chaos and pulling something out of it. The child colouring a page and the architect designing a building are the same archetype.
shadowAspect: Perfectionism, blocks, comparison. Saying 'I'm not good enough' and never starting. Or merging with the creation and dying from criticism.
dreamMeaning: Painting, playing music, building, giving birth, planting a garden in a dream — all the Creator archetype. A blank canvas / page is a sign that you are ready for the invitation.
wakingMeaning: If you have not created lately you feel an inner pressure — part of what we call depression is this pressure. As soon as you produce, it eases. The output doesn't have to be good; the flow itself heals.
advice: Today for 10 minutes do something with no goal: scribble, hum, improvise. Give up the outcome and let process flow.
affirmation: Creating is my nature. I have the right to create without being perfect.

name: Orphan (Ordinary Person)
essence: The Orphan archetype is born when paradise is lost. The moment of 'life is not what they said'. Consciousness realising the saviour is not coming. It hurts, but it is the gate of maturation.
lightAspect: Empathy, solidarity, realism. Meeting those who suffer the same. Letting go of false hopes and building real relationships.
shadowAspect: Victim mentality. 'It always happens to me, nobody understands.' Losing agency, constantly waiting for a saviour, sulkiness.
dreamMeaning: Being alone in a dream, being in an orphanage, losing your parents, being abandoned — the voice of the Orphan. Usually arrives after a disappointment, the collapse of an expectation.
wakingMeaning: You trusted someone and didn't get what you expected, a dream collapsed, an illusion dissolved. Bitter truth, but growing. Accepting that you are an orphan is the start of reclaiming your own agency.
advice: Today accept that 'no one will come' — then ask yourself: so what can I do? A life without a saviour is actually a free life.
affirmation: I do not wait for a saviour. I own my own authority.

name: Rebel
essence: The Rebel archetype is the energy that feels the injustice of the system and stands against it. Prometheus steals fire, Spartacus breaks chains. The inner voice that says 'this shouldn't be this way'.
lightAspect: Justice, freedom, transformation. The collapse of aging structures, room for the new. Art movements, revolutions, personal moltings.
shadowAspect: Destruction in the name of anarchy. Being against everything but not knowing what you want. Seeing authority as a personal enemy. Constantly angry, constantly fighting.
dreamMeaning: Standing against a system in dreams, escaping prison, joining a revolution, saying 'no' to authority — Rebel archetype signals. Something inside wants to explode.
wakingMeaning: If you are constantly uncomfortable at work, in a relationship or at home, the Rebel is speaking. The question to ask: does the system really need to change, or is it a bond within me?
advice: Today question a rule you follow. 'Who set this? Does it still serve me?' Take a small piece of freedom each day.
affirmation: I have the right to resist. My rebellion is sacred when it is in service.

name: Innocent
essence: The Innocent archetype believes the world is good. The child's first trust, the lover's first admiration, the believer's first faith. Nothing starts without this trust.
lightAspect: Hope, openness, love, the ability to begin again. The eye that can see light even in the darkest place. Gratitude and wonder.
shadowAspect: Naivety, denial, not seeing reality. Saying 'all will be well' and refusing danger. Open to deception, fertile ground for manipulation.
dreamMeaning: Gardens, white clothes, a small child, an angel, an open sky — heralds of the Innocent. After a crisis the message is 'start again, the trust remains'.
wakingMeaning: Can you still rejoice like a child? Can you accept someone's kindness first? If you look at everything with suspicion the Innocent is repressed. This contracts life.
advice: Today trust something without reason. Get caught in the rain, buy a gift for a stranger, tell a dream like a child.
affirmation: I have the right to trust life. My innocence is not stupidity.

name: Seeker
essence: The Seeker archetype cannot fit inside; it runs to the horizon. It asks 'is that all?' and looks for more. Explorer, pilgrim, philosopher, wanderer — its faces.
lightAspect: Expansion, opening horizons, being after truth. Courage to break the comfort zone. Seeing life as a journey.
shadowAspect: Cannot stay anywhere, constantly running, afraid to commit. 'There's something better' and unable to value what's in hand.
dreamMeaning: Travelling in dreams, looking for a map, being in a foreign city, climbing a mountain, the ship en route — the Seeker. Something is calling you.
wakingMeaning: If you feel something is missing but cannot ask 'what?', the Seeker has awakened. The answer is not in the future, but inside continuing to ask the question.
advice: Today open a notebook: 'What am I seeking in my life?' Write uncensored for 10 minutes. Let the words be your map.
affirmation: Seeking is my nature. Not finding is not a failure but an invitation.

name: Ruler
essence: The Ruler archetype is the energy of the mature king/queen — the inner maturity that can govern their own kingdom. Just, fertile, responsible, holding space.
lightAspect: Taking responsibility, looking after others, setting vision. The mature leader decides not for their own ego but for everyone in the field.
shadowAspect: Tyrant (control, bullying) or weak king (refusing responsibility, fleeing). Neither is genuine authority.
dreamMeaning: Dreams of crowns, thrones, palaces, kings, queens, being at the head of a ceremony are Ruler signals. If you dream of a deposed king an old authority figure may be falling.
wakingMeaning: People in your life are waiting for decisions, direction — the Ruler's call. If you run away, chaos; if you embrace it, maturity. Your own life is your kingdom.
advice: Today review three 'kingdoms' (health, relationships, work). Ask: 'If I were the king/queen of this area, what would I do?'
affirmation: I am the owner of my own kingdom. Responsibility does not limit my power, it opens it.

name: Healer
essence: The Healer archetype is the wounded healer. From Asclepius to the Shaman, from physician to therapist — healing usually comes from the one who knows their own wound.
lightAspect: Whoever can tend their wound does not wound; they can sit in another's pain. Treatment is not just technique, it is a matter of presence.
shadowAspect: Rescuer behaviour — running from your own wound while trying to save others. Feeding off sickness, unable to truly heal anyone. Both sickened and sickening.
dreamMeaning: Physician, hospital, gathering herbs, bandaging your own wound, caring for someone — Healer signs. An area is ready to heal.
wakingMeaning: Your circle waits for you to heal them, and you wait to be healed by yourself. Healing is continuity and gentleness; it doesn't happen in one session. First know your own wound.
advice: Today recognise a wound (betrayal, loss, criticism). Do not try to close it; just put a hand on it and say 'I see you'.
affirmation: I know my wound and through it I can heal another.

name: Magician
essence: The Magician archetype can transform reality with consciousness. Uses intent, ritual, symbol. The modern magician may be a scientist, engineer, therapist — the essence is conscious transformation.
lightAspect: Changing inner and outer reality through the power of intent. Quantum, ritual, therapy — the form doesn't matter, the principle is the same.
shadowAspect: Manipulation, black magic, gathering power by deceiving others. One step further is the manipulative advertiser, demagogue, dead cult leader.
dreamMeaning: Doing magic in dreams, lighting fires, seeing symbols, changing words — the Magician calling. A transformation seed is inside you.
wakingMeaning: If what you intended in your life has started happening, if synchronicities have increased, the Magician is awake. The opposite — 'nothing is changing' — is misalignment between intent and action.
advice: Today write a clear intent, do a small ritual (light a candle, drink water, deep breath), release into your life. Symbols engage the unconscious.
affirmation: When my intent and action align, my world transforms.

name: Jester / Fool
essence: The court jester was the only person who could tell the truth even to the king. Humour carried the reality the king could not accept. The fool digests wisdom in jest.
lightAspect: Telling the truth with humour, easing tension, being able to laugh at yourself, embracing the absurdity of life.
shadowAspect: Constantly joking and not taking anything seriously. Covering pain with a mask. Using humour as a cruel weapon.
dreamMeaning: Clowns, stages, laughter, changing costumes, falling yourself — Jester signals. Life is warning you 'don't take this so seriously'.
wakingMeaning: Has something become too heavy? Ground for the Jester. A laugh, an absurd anecdote, self-mockery can crack the whole structure.
advice: Today narrate the most serious thing on your mind like a comedian. The structure inflates and pops.
affirmation: By laughing at myself I approach truth.

name: Virgin / Maiden
essence: The mythic virgin is not 'sexually virgin' but 'belonging to herself'. Artemis bows to no one, Athena chooses her path. Whole without taking a man as her axis.
lightAspect: Independence, choosing your path, inner wholeness. Being full even when alone. Not needing another's approval.
shadowAspect: Fear of bonding, cold loneliness, refusal of intimacy. In the shadow the emotional wall is thick.
dreamMeaning: Spring gardens, white flowers, a lone forest walk, a woman in the woods — Virgin signs. Something says 'find yourself again'.
wakingMeaning: Time to remember a 'you' independent of your relational role. Not mother, not wife, not child — who are you? The Virgin calls back to that root.
advice: Today make an hour yours alone: don't share, don't post to social media, just keep it for yourself.
affirmation: I am not alone, I am with myself. Belonging to me is fullness.

name: Crone / Witch
essence: The witch archetype — Hecate, Baba Yaga, Morrigan. The old, wise, independent woman feared by society. 'Witch hunts' fought against this wisdom in history.
lightAspect: Deep knowledge of nature, intuitive healing, the moon cycle, the death-rebirth cycle. Fully her own.
shadowAspect: Jealousy, cursing, social judgement. The bitterness of being cast out by society.
dreamMeaning: Old woman, forest hut, cauldron, black cat, moon — Witch voice. Repressed feminine wisdom wants to speak.
wakingMeaning: Your inner 'crazy', 'dangerous', 'too much' side — these are the Witch. Wisdom repressed for social approval. Recognise her; do not give up being civil, but do not kill the wild woman inside.
advice: Today spend a conscious moment with a nature element (fire, water, plant). Whisper an intention. This is modern witchcraft.
affirmation: I respect the old wise woman inside me. I do not allow her to be judged.

name: Warrior
essence: The mature warrior has their own discipline, protects their boundaries, knows their purpose. The sword doesn't cut outside, only when required; most of the time it cuts your own slackness.
lightAspect: Discipline, endurance, boundary, focus. Doing what you promised, investing in the body through difficulty.
shadowAspect: Aggressive, militant, turning everything into war. Or passive — fleeing fights, unable to set boundaries. Neither is mature.
dreamMeaning: Fighting, training, holding a sword, being a soldier — Warrior signs. A call for resolve in some area.
wakingMeaning: If you know what you want lately but can't apply it — the Warrior is asleep. Discipline isn't because you love it, it's a need.
advice: Today set a small discipline: get up 10 minutes early, 5 push-ups, say one 'no'. Will is a muscle — used, it strengthens.
affirmation: I draw my boundaries. I do what I promise.

name: Saint / Eren
essence: The Saint / Eren archetype is the person devoted to a great truth. Hallac, Yunus, St Francis. They have given their ego to the service of the 'greater'.
lightAspect: Devotion, humility, service, inner peace. Seeing the small as sacred. Gratitude.
shadowAspect: Spiritual bypass, denying real feelings, false humility, turning self-renunciation into masochism.
dreamMeaning: Praying, seeing light, dervish, church, temple, being in worship — Saint signs. Something calls you to 'rise'.
wakingMeaning: If life has lost meaning, if you seek an inner direction, the Saint archetype is ready to open. But beware: true devotion is not an escape from the world, but another way of turning toward it.
advice: Today sit silently for 5 minutes. Wordless. Don't ask 'who am I?' — just sit. This small worship is the saint's path.
affirmation: I am part of something larger than me. Service sets me free.

name: Hunter / Eater
essence: The hunter / eater archetype is the primal body, hunger, instinct. Not always 'negative' — you need hunger to survive. Repressed, it bursts out.
lightAspect: Natural appetite, instinctual intelligence, body wisdom, seeing opportunity. The hunter knows what to hunt and when to wait.
shadowAspect: Uncontrolled hunger — eating disorders, addictions, sexual obsessions, hunting others. Modern life disconnected from the body distorts this archetype.
dreamMeaning: Hunting, being hunted, a wild animal, being eaten, chasing food — Hunter signs. A primal need is calling you.
wakingMeaning: If you suppress your appetite long enough (food, touch, movement, passion) this archetype grows in the dark. See it; then the rabid hunter calms.
advice: Today ask a real bodily need: am I hungry? thirsty? tired? The answer is usually not what it appears, but listening to the body is the beginning.
affirmation: I know the wild one inside me. I feed them civilly.

name: Father / Generative Father
essence: The Great Father archetype — Zeus, sky gods, inner authority. The one who sets the rule, gives the structure, leaves the legacy. The masculine counterpart of the Great Mother.
lightAspect: Giving direction, preparing the child for the world, protecting, setting boundaries. The solid voice that says 'you can do it'.
shadowAspect: Authoritarian father — intimidating, conditional love. Or absent father — leaving, indifferent. Both extremes operate internally too.
dreamMeaning: Father, mountain, the figure looking down, sky god, authority — Father call. You are invited to bond with your inner authority.
wakingMeaning: If you struggle with the structural decisions of life (career, investment, direction), the inner father bond is weak. Reconciling with him (real father included or not) opens this door.
advice: Today write a sentence to your inner father: 'What do I want to learn from you?' Wait for the answer, don't force.
affirmation: I own my inner authority. Structure is not crushing.

name: Wounded Healer
essence: Chiron the centaur, immortal, carries a wound that never heals — yet from that very wound he heals others. The modern therapist, healer, mentor.
lightAspect: The one who accepts their wound and turns it into service. Empathy comes from deep, because they know their own pain. Authentic and effective guidance.
shadowAspect: Always talking from their own wound but it never closes. Actually projecting their pain while 'helping'. Exhausted from looking after both themselves and others.
dreamMeaning: An old wound, its mark, a centaur (half-man, half-horse), an unhealing cut, but bandaging another — Wounded Healer signs.
wakingMeaning: Have you carried your pain somewhere useful? Or do you keep reopening the wound you try to close? This archetype reminds you the wound is a gift.
advice: Today think of a past pain. What do you know from that pain today? How can you give that knowledge to someone else?
affirmation: Owning my wound is the beginning of owning another.

name: Maiden / Kore
essence: Persephone (Kore) is a girl, abducted, descending to the underworld, becoming queen. The pure maiden earns her deep womanhood through the underworld journey. The archetype of transition.
lightAspect: The side that returns from descent: depth, knowing both life and death, the queen who can live in two worlds.
shadowAspect: Victim position — 'it happened without my knowledge, I am innocent'. Or staying stuck in the underworld, depression, going inward.
dreamMeaning: Underground, kidnap, dark passage, being pulled while picking flowers — Kore. The painful part of maturing is at the door.
wakingMeaning: Has an area of your life collapsed? Have you been drawn into unconsciousness? This is the Kore journey. The underworld is not bad — there you learn to be queen.
advice: Today don't resist the 'darkness' you feel. Sit, stay, watch. Soften it with 'this too is transition'. Without autumn there is no spring.
affirmation: Going down to darkness makes me deep, not abstract.

name: Trickster God (Coyote, Hermes)
essence: Coyote, Hermes, Anansi — gods who create through chaos. They build the world by breaking rules; they begin not from order but from play.
lightAspect: Creative chaos, transformative humour, transgressing boundaries, forming new connections.
shadowAspect: Not standing behind your word, theft, lies, manipulation. Off-balance trickster creates destruction.
dreamMeaning: An animal-human figure, chaos scene, theft, multiple faces, surprising turns — Hermes/Coyote at work. The unconscious says 'let's loosen the strict order'.
wakingMeaning: If unexpected events, surprise meetings, lost items, accidental encounters multiply, Hermes is walking. Lucky coincidences come from here.
advice: Today disrupt your plan a little. Coincide. Hermes's door opens in unplannedness.
affirmation: Order serves me, I do not serve order. Play is sacred too.

name: Mother Goddess (Demeter, Isis)
essence: Demeter, Isis, Cybele — creative goddesses. Giving life, taking life, carrying the cycle. Larger than motherhood: the world itself.
lightAspect: Abundance, protection, creation, knowing of the cycle. Both birthing and mourning, both strength and surrender.
shadowAspect: Frozen in mourning (Demeter dries the world while looking for her daughter). Controlling, owning the child / creation.
dreamMeaning: Fertile fields, harvest, looking for a lost child, grain, a mother-goddess figure — voice of this archetype. A cycle is closing or opening.
wakingMeaning: If you have suffered a loss (person, project, health) this archetype's mourning side is active. Without mourning, abundance does not return. Mourning is the last form of love.
advice: Today name a loss. Writing or speaking is the same. Saying 'I miss you' opens the way to abundance.
affirmation: Even when mourning my abundance does not diminish. I trust the cycle.

name: Couple / Hieros Gamos
essence: Hieros Gamos — the sacred marriage. The inner union of masculine and feminine, sky and earth, consciousness and unconscious. Alchemy describes it as the chemical wedding.
lightAspect: Inner wholeness, balance, creative tension, deep relationships. Unions built on equality.
shadowAspect: Fanatical attachment to outer union, the 'half orange' fallacy, looking for inner wholeness outside.
dreamMeaning: Wedding in a dream, a couple, marrying, two figures uniting, king-queen — Hieros Gamos. Symbol of inner integration.
wakingMeaning: If a relationship is growing you internally (not just companionship but transformation) this energy is open. Or if even alone you are reconciling your inner masculine-feminine.
advice: Today put your inner masculine (structure, will) and feminine (flow, intuition) in dialogue on a page. 'What do you want?' Don't compare answers; listen.
affirmation: I unite two poles inside me. Wholeness is inside before outside.

name: Adversary / Antagonist
essence: Every myth has an enemy testing the hero. Actually not external but the hero's shadow externalised. Without an enemy the hero does not develop.
lightAspect: Resistance and obstacle in life, necessary for growth. The enemy's presence shows you what you defend.
shadowAspect: Constantly creating enemies, paranoia, externalising mental structure. Saying 'because of that man' for every problem of life.
dreamMeaning: Seeing the enemy in a dream, being chased, fighting, a scary figure — Adversary signs. The unconscious asks 'which side of me is this?'
wakingMeaning: If you have an 'enemy' figure — boss, ex, relative — ask: what does that person reflect in me? Most of the time they confront you with a trait you suppressed.
advice: Today ask if an 'enemy' has a counterpart in you. 'If that trait were also in me, where would it hide?'
affirmation: My enemy may be a mirror. Before attacking, I look.

name: Sister Goddesses (Three Sisters)
essence: The triple goddess — maiden (potential), mother (creation), crone (wisdom). The Moirai (Clotho-Lachesis-Atropos) spin, measure, cut the thread of life.
lightAspect: Acceptance of three stages of life, map of inner maturing, the dance of fate and will.
shadowAspect: Getting stuck in one stage (eternal girl, smother-mother, bitter crone). Refusing to age or to see the future.
dreamMeaning: Three women, three figures, the thread of fate, an age change, a triple mirror — signs of the triple goddess. There is a transition in your life stage.
wakingMeaning: If you are moving between life phases (youth-midlife-wisdom) this archetype is open. Time to reconcile with body and role.
advice: Today ask honestly which stage you are in. 'Which woman is dominant within?' All three can be there, just let them balance.
affirmation: I honour all three of my cycles.

name: Healing Child / Holy Child
essence: The Holy Child archetype — Jesus, Krishna, Mithra. Light born in darkness. The start of a new cycle, purity born within.
lightAspect: Renewal, inspiration, a sense of miracle, fresh start. Light born inside the darkness — 'there is still hope'.
shadowAspect: False messianism, childhood romanticism, refusing responsibility. 'I am so special, let them wait'.
dreamMeaning: Birth of a baby, a luminous child, a holy family, a stable, a crib — the Holy Child. Something new is being born — a project, an intention, a transformation.
wakingMeaning: Before a creative birth you feel inner pressure. This archetype arrives then. Don't fear — your new state is cleaner.
advice: Today write a long-bud intention. Say 'you are being born'. Begin to protect it.
affirmation: Something new is born inside me. I open space for it.

name: Lost / Exile
essence: The exile archetype — Odysseus, Moses, Hagar. Cast away, waiting for years to return, or finding things changed on return. The home-universe shifts.
lightAspect: Exile deepens identity; when you don't belong you learn who you are. The return is a re-choice.
shadowAspect: Constantly moving, never bonding, losing the sense of home. Or the opposite: obsessive return, even when the old place is gone.
dreamMeaning: Being in a foreign place, getting lost, unable to go home, your luggage not opening — Exile signs. There is a 'cannot belong' feeling in some area.
wakingMeaning: If a 'home' collapsed — place, job, relationship — and the new one isn't clear yet, you are in this archetype. The in-between is precious; do not rush to fill it.
advice: Today ask 'where is home?' In the body, in a person, in a memory? Home is first an inner feeling, then a place.
affirmation: My exile is also my journey. Home travels inside me.

name: Jealous God / Old King
essence: Cronus eats his children because he fears losing his throne. The old king / jealous god archetype is immature authority that refuses to make room.
lightAspect: If recognised internally: 'where am I not making room?' Opening to rejuvenation and handing over.
shadowAspect: Not making room for the new generation, new idea, partner. Micromanagement, envying others' success, clinging tightly to one's seat.
dreamMeaning: Being deposed from the throne, devouring children / power, a besieged fortress — the Old King. You may be blocking a younger one — or being blocked.
wakingMeaning: Does an honest pang of resentment come at a younger one's success/joy? Or a senior's at yours? This archetype is active. Recognise, start there.
advice: Today say a sincere compliment to someone above/below you. Making room is not losing but renewing.
affirmation: I make room. Another's rise is not my fall.

name: Underworld Traveller (Orpheus, Inanna)
essence: Orpheus, Inanna, Demeter — descend consciously to the underworld and return. Modern psychology's 'shadow work' is this ancient journey.
lightAspect: Conscious descent, encountering the shadow, retrieving the lost piece, wisdom returning from the depths.
shadowAspect: Not returning (chronic depression, the inverse of spiritual bypass), or never descending at all (a surface life).
dreamMeaning: Descending into a cave / underworld, seeing the dead, a dark corridor, an elevator going down — Descent signs. The unconscious is calling.
wakingMeaning: A crisis, loss, depression or genuine therapy process — modern forms of the underworld journey. You will return, but know the one who returns will not be you.
advice: Today say 'hello' to a side that feels dark. Healing is not in fleeing the darkness but in sitting there. But not alone — with a guide, therapist, friend.
affirmation: I descend and return. The bottom of the dark is the start of rebirth.

name: Shaman
essence: The shaman is a bridge between the visible and invisible realms. They fall ill, die, return — they carry their wound for the community's healing. The ancient form of the wounded healer.
lightAspect: Inter-dimensional mediator, dream interpreter, community balancer. Modern equivalents: deep therapist, creative artist, spiritual guide.
shadowAspect: False shaman — selling 'guidance' without knowledge, using others' wounds, ego trip.
dreamMeaning: Flying in dreams, talking with animals, being among spirits, drum sounds, around fire — Shaman signs. The unconscious is noticing itself inter-dimensionally.
wakingMeaning: If your circle leans on your 'deep seeing' side, or if you carry your own wound's knowledge to another, this archetype is active in you. The responsibility is great.
advice: Today write a dream. Taste a breath. Be open to the unseen — one hour is enough.
affirmation: I am a bridge between two worlds. I exist insofar as I can serve.

name: Death and Return of the God-King
essence: The dying-and-returning god (Osiris, Tammuz, Dionysus, Jesus) — the deep rhythm of life. Every ending is the seed of resurrection. On the personal scale: shedding skin.
lightAspect: Teaches to release the old state, to trust the new birth. Acceptance of seasonal / biological / emotional deaths.
shadowAspect: Messiah complex, constantly playing the 'sacrifice' role, the drama of dying for others, refusing rebirth.
dreamMeaning: Dying in dreams (especially seeing yourself dead), tomb, resurrection, seasonal change, dead loved ones returning — this archetype. A cycle closes; do not fear.
wakingMeaning: An old identity (job, relationship, role) is collapsing while the new isn't clear — resurrection from this 'dead' field is near. A moment of patience and surrender.
advice: Today consciously let something 'end' — a habit, a group, a fear. Make room. The resurrection field awaits preparation.
affirmation: Dying and being reborn is my natural law. I am not afraid, I trust.

name: Mature Elder / Pole
essence: The pole, the axis of the world; in Sufism the 'pole of the time', in Jung the completed Self. The final figure where all archetypes integrate. Silent, full, giving direction through presence.
lightAspect: Integrated opposites, ego and Self reconciled, inner maturity communicating without need of words.
shadowAspect: False pole — the ego that says 'I am the pole'. The true pole never speaks; recognition belongs to others.
dreamMeaning: The summit of a mountain in a dream, a wise old hodja, a figure surrounded by light, the centre of a mandala — Pole signs. A large step on the individuation path.
wakingMeaning: If you can hold inner balance with outer events at once lately, if what once hurt is now only information, the pole approaches. Not a goal — a becoming.
advice: Today look at your life with the eyes of an 80-year-old sage. What would they say to this event? That voice is already inside you.
affirmation: The end of the road is my road. I am open to maturing.

name: Fool (Tarot 0)
essence: Tarot's zero card — the start of the whole journey. The Fool stands at the edge of the cliff but is not afraid; he has not yet learned to judge. The pure state of the soul, freshly born consciousness.
lightAspect: Courage, openness, unexpected leap. Opening a clean page in a new chapter. Taking a step without calculation.
shadowAspect: Foolishness, carelessness, not seeing consequences. Walking off the same cliff repeatedly. Refusing to mature.
dreamMeaning: Standing at a cliff's edge in dreams, setting out with a bundle of clothes, a small dog following you — Fool archetype. The unconscious invites you to a new cycle.
wakingMeaning: You are opening a 'fresh page' in some area. Not knowing is scary, but the Fool says: you couldn't have known without taking the first step. Not risk — beginning.
advice: Today pick a small 'I don't know' experience. A new path, a new taste, a new conversation. Purity rotates.
affirmation: Not knowing is also knowledge. I have the right to start from zero.

name: High Priestess (Tarot II)
essence: The High Priestess sits between two pillars — known and unknown, night and day. The Torah in her hand, a moon crown on her head. The feminine voice carrying inner knowledge.
lightAspect: Intuition, inner knowing, silent wisdom. Hearing what hasn't been said. The capacity to read messages from dreams, body and nature.
shadowAspect: Not sharing the secret, cold distance, distrust of one's own intuition. The pride that hides what it knows.
dreamMeaning: A library, an altar veiled with a curtain, a woman under moonlight, an old book — High Priestess. Inner knowledge wants to emerge.
wakingMeaning: When making a decision, if you cannot explain the reason but an inner voice still gives direction, the High Priestess is speaking. Confirm with logic, but don't reject the first voice.
advice: Today write the intuition you have on a decision, in a single sentence. Add the reason later. The order matters.
affirmation: I know what I don't know. I trust my intuition.

name: Empress (Tarot III)
essence: The Empress sits on the throne of nature's abundance, surrounded by wheat and running water. The concrete, world-visible form of creative feminine energy.
lightAspect: Abundance, creation, love, body wisdom. Harmony with nature. Opening space for art, child, project to be born.
shadowAspect: Overprotective, smothering motherhood. Being a slave to material things. Making only bodily pleasure into meaning.
dreamMeaning: Pregnancy in a dream, planting a garden, fertile fields, wearing a crown — the Empress. Something inside is about to be born — project, relationship, self-renewal.
wakingMeaning: You are in the middle of a creative process. Your body, your space, your relationships are fertile. The Empress says: let it flow, don't block.
advice: Today 'create' something — a meal, a craft, a letter, an idea. Creating is meditation.
affirmation: I have the right to create. Abundance flows through me.

name: Emperor (Tarot IV)
essence: The Emperor sits on his stone-carved throne. Firmness, structure, rule. Mature masculine authority — the boundary and order that won't be torn down.
lightAspect: Responsibility, boundary, protection, building structure. The maturity that can say 'no'. Inner authority.
shadowAspect: Tyrant, control freak, loveless discipline. Not paternal but oppressive. Slave to the rule.
dreamMeaning: Respecting a king/commander in a dream, the throne room, rules carved in stone — the Emperor. Time to put structure into an area.
wakingMeaning: If an area of your life is scattered the Emperor says: first draw the boundary, then talk freedom. Love deepens within structure.
advice: Today set a clear rule in one area. For yourself, not someone else. Structure is the precondition for freedom.
affirmation: I have the right to set boundaries. My authority is in service, not in pressure.

name: Hierophant (Tarot V)
essence: The Hierophant is the bridge carrying sacred knowledge through tradition. Sheikh, hoja, priest, scholar — one who passes knowledge to a generation.
lightAspect: Wisdom of tradition, the power of continuity, the humility of learning. Teaching, mentorship, carrying legacy.
shadowAspect: Dogma, being a slave to the institution, not questioning. Using knowledge for control. Saying 'untraditional' to a new voice.
dreamMeaning: A hoja / sheikh / priest in a dream, a sacred ceremony, a lesson from an old book — Hierophant. A teaching wants to share with you.
wakingMeaning: Is a tradition, lineage, master approaching? The Hierophant says: there is power in tradition but it is not dogma. Your right to ask is preserved.
advice: Today think of a legacy from a tradition (family, work, culture) that comes toward you. Own it — not blindly, consciously.
affirmation: I respect tradition but not blindly. Criticism is part of acceptance.

name: Chariot (Tarot VII)
essence: The Chariot is pulled by two sphinxes (light and dark). The driver steers not by reins but by inner alignment. The pure form of will.
lightAspect: Focus on a goal, inner will, carrying victory. Succeeding in pulling opposing forces in one direction.
shadowAspect: Obsession, over-control, sacrificing everything for the win. Losing direction but still driving.
dreamMeaning: Riding horses in a dream, driving a chariot, being in a race — the Chariot. You are pulling all your energy toward one goal.
wakingMeaning: In some area you are channelling all your energy to one point. Is it the right direction? You'll ask when you reach the summit. For now reins are in your hand.
advice: Today pull back the energy you scatter. Write three priorities; postpone the rest a week. Focus is power.
affirmation: I set my direction. My will unites opposites.

name: Strength (Tarot VIII)
essence: A woman gently closes the jaw of a wild lion — not by force. Real strength is not control, but recognising and taming with gentleness.
lightAspect: Patience, inner calm, consciously carrying the wild side. Not force but the power of love. Walking gently despite fear.
shadowAspect: Repressed anger, false softness. Denying the wild and fleeing oneself. Or the opposite — a slave to control.
dreamMeaning: Calming a wild animal in a dream, touching it, walking with it — Strength. You are making peace with the wild side inside you.
wakingMeaning: In a triggered moment can you choose gentleness? The Strength archetype opens precisely here. Not reaction — response.
advice: Today if anger comes, wait 30 seconds. Then speak. This patience is the door of strength.
affirmation: Love is the greatest strength. I recognise the wild one inside me.

name: Hermit (Tarot IX)
essence: The Hermit is alone on a mountain, lantern in hand. Carries knowledge not from the crowd but from silence. Deep knowing is impossible without solitude.
lightAspect: Conscious solitude, inner quest, carrying one's own light. Wisdom not found in crowds.
shadowAspect: Fleeing into loneliness and rejecting bonds, social withdrawal, spiritual pride. 'I understood, no one understands.'
dreamMeaning: On a high mountain in a dream, in a cave, walking with a lantern, a deep library — Hermit. A call coming from within.
wakingMeaning: Has your desire to socialise decreased? The need to be alone is normal — Hermit energy is temporary but real. Don't curse connection and leave; just turn inward.
advice: Today practice 1 hour of 'no social media, no talking'. Notice your own light.
affirmation: My solitude is not escape but deepening.

name: Wheel of Fortune (Tarot X)
essence: The Wheel of Fortune spins continuously. Yesterday at the top, today at the bottom; today at the bottom, tomorrow turns again. The fundamental rhythm of life: change.
lightAspect: Acceptance of flow, knowing the moment is temporary. Being calm in both victory and loss.
shadowAspect: Staying in the wheel's grip, refusing control entirely. Or the opposite — exhausting yourself trying to stop the wheel.
dreamMeaning: A wheel in a dream, being on a Ferris wheel, going up and down — Wheel. A cycle is turning — don't resist, watch.
wakingMeaning: Your life is at a point — peak or pit. The Wheel says: it is not permanent. This too shall pass. Both comforting and warning.
advice: Today recall a 'peak' and a 'pit' moment. Both passed. This too will pass.
affirmation: Change is natural. Instead of resisting the wheel, I dance with it.

name: Justice (Tarot XI)
essence: Justice — sword in one hand, scales in the other. Eyes open — no bargaining, only weighing. The personified law of cause and effect.
lightAspect: Honesty, cause-effect awareness, taking responsibility. Aligning what you say with what you do.
shadowAspect: Judgement, revenge, cold calculation. Turning justice into personal anger.
dreamMeaning: Scales in a dream, a courtroom, a judge, a decision moment — Justice. An account is closing — inner or outer.
wakingMeaning: In some area you have an 'account' — with yourself or another. Justice says: face it. Postponing the bargain causes damage.
advice: Today is there an 'account' you've postponed? (Debt, apology, contract, honest conversation.) Take one small step.
affirmation: My responsibility gives me freedom. I close my account myself.

name: Hanged Man (Tarot XII)
essence: The Hanged Man hangs by his foot, upside down — yet his face is calm. Conscious surrender, pause, seeing the world in reverse.
lightAspect: Sacrifice of ego, seeing from a reverse angle, conscious waiting. The power of 'not doing'.
shadowAspect: Victim role, victimhood, getting stuck in the inversion. Wilting in passive resistance.
dreamMeaning: Hanging upside down in a dream, a reversed view, seeing the world as if turned over — Hanged Man. Time to change your view in some area.
wakingMeaning: If something is annoying you, the solution may not be 'do more' — suspend it. Look from another angle. Waiting is a decision.
advice: Today step back 24 hours from what you are insisting on solving. When you return you'll see it has shifted.
affirmation: Waiting is also action. My surrender is not weakness.

name: Death (Tarot XIII)
essence: Death in Tarot is not physical death — it is the end of a chapter. Without letting go of the old, the new does not arrive. It looks scary but it is the most healing card.
lightAspect: Courage to end, releasing, opening space for renewal. When a worn-out part dies the true rebirth begins.
shadowAspect: Refusing endings, artificially extending a dead relationship/job/identity. Or the opposite — endlessly destroying and starting over, with nothing rooting.
dreamMeaning: Seeing your own death (lying in a coffin, last breath), an old cemetery, an ex being buried — Death. Don't be afraid — this card heralds the healthiest transformation.
wakingMeaning: An area of life is officially 'ending'. Mourn but don't cling. When you make space, the new arrives. This card is most often misunderstood.
advice: Today officially end something. Cancel a subscription, mute a group, drop a habit. Small death, big space.
affirmation: I allow endings. What needed to die was not my growth.

name: Temperance (Tarot XIV)
essence: An angel pours water between two cups — neither too much nor too little, continuous flow. Temperance: opposites meeting in the right ratio.
lightAspect: Patience, balance, the right ratio. Knowing how much of what. Not running to extremes.
shadowAspect: Passivity, never reaching either extreme, indecision. Surrendering initiative under the excuse of 'temperance'.
dreamMeaning: Mixing in a dream, pouring water, combining two things — Temperance. Something inside is looking for the 'right proportion'.
wakingMeaning: Have you swung to extremes in an area? (Work, relationship, eating, feeling.) Temperance says: find the middle, not with one move — with small adjustments.
advice: Today make a 10% 'correction' to an area you've over-pushed. Neither too much nor too little — a small re-alignment.
affirmation: I can live without going to extremes. I know the right ratio.

name: Devil (Tarot XV)
essence: On the Devil card two people are bound with a chain — but the chain is loose. Captivity mostly comes from inside. Symbol of addiction, obsession, captive passion.
lightAspect: Seeing where you're stuck. Realising the chain is loose. Approaching lust without judgement.
shadowAspect: Addiction (substance, relationship, work, social media). Romanticising the dark. The 'I can't' narrative.
dreamMeaning: Being chained in a dream, surrendering to a dark figure, unable to escape — Devil. An addiction or obsession invites you — to face it.
wakingMeaning: In some area do you say 'I can't'? The Devil says: look at the chain — is it really locked? Often it isn't.
advice: Today try for 24 hours to do what you say 'I can't'. Just 24 hours. Test the chain.
affirmation: I know my addictions. I own my chain — and my exit.

name: Tower (Tarot XVI)
essence: The Tower is struck by lightning and falls; everything inside drops. The sudden collapse of everything built on falseness. Shaking but freeing.
lightAspect: Fall of the false structure, the truth becoming visible. The relief of 'it wasn't that much anyway'.
shadowAspect: Romanticising destruction, deliberately burning your own tower. Chaos for chaos's sake.
dreamMeaning: A building collapsing in a dream, lightning striking, an earthquake — Tower. An area is about to change suddenly; don't fear, that part was false.
wakingMeaning: Have you had an unexpected loss, exposure, collapse? The Tower shakes but takes away what didn't belong. Your structure will be rebuilt.
advice: Today recall your last 'shaking' experience. What false comfort did that event take away? What is real there now?
affirmation: The fallen structure did not belong. The new foundation will be firmer.

name: Star (Tarot XVII)
essence: A naked woman under the night sky pours water onto both earth and pool, with 8 stars above her. Calm hope arriving after a crisis.
lightAspect: Renewed hope, inspiration, openness. The return of light after darkness. Real gratitude.
shadowAspect: Naive optimism, dissociation from reality, the 'think well and it will be well' romance.
dreamMeaning: Peace under a starry sky in a dream, sitting by a pond, pouring water — Star. Healing has begun after a crisis.
wakingMeaning: Have you emerged from a hard period? Star says: now breathe. Not every wound may be closed yet, but hope is real.
advice: Today look at the night sky (or a starry photo) for 5 minutes. Allow yourself unreasoned hope.
affirmation: The darkness passed. I have the right to renew.

name: Moon (Tarot XVIII)
essence: Under the moon a wolf and a dog look, a crab emerges from water. Uncertainty, dream, the territory of the unconscious. Not bright but important.
lightAspect: Intuitive depth, dream work, descending to the unconscious. Accepting uncertainty.
shadowAspect: Illusion, paranoia, deciding from fear. Confusing darkness with reality.
dreamMeaning: Walking under the moon in a dream, a shadowy view, howling animals — Moon. Something is processing in the unconscious — it will take time to clarify.
wakingMeaning: If everything isn't clear now, don't panic. Moon says: you must pass through the illusion. Clarity comes later.
advice: Today instead of clear decisions, write a dream. There is information in uncertainty.
affirmation: I tolerate uncertainty. I do not confuse darkness with knowledge.

name: Sun (Tarot XIX)
essence: On the Sun card a child rides a white horse in front of sunflowers, sun blazing above. Pure joy, success, the genuine beauty of life.
lightAspect: Joy, clarity, real success, playful spirit. Rejoicing in what is to be rejoiced.
shadowAspect: False optimism, not seeing the shadow, the constant pressure to 'be good'.
dreamMeaning: Bright sun in a dream, a field of sunflowers, a happy child — Sun. Earned clarity is coming in some area.
wakingMeaning: Are you in a good period? Sun says: own it. Don't damage the present moment with 'one day this will end'. Now is real.
advice: Today live a 'good' moment consciously. No photo, no extension, no commentary — just be.
affirmation: I have the right to joy. Denying goodness is not humility.

name: Judgement (Tarot XX)
essence: An angel blows a trumpet, the dead rise from graves. Awakening to a call, facing the past, the decision of rebirth.
lightAspect: Listening to a genuine call, honouring the past, awakening to a new life.
shadowAspect: Getting stuck in the past, a judgemental approach, the rhetoric of false 'awakening'.
dreamMeaning: The sound of a trumpet in a dream, old loved ones calling, rising from a grave — Judgement. A call is rising from within.
wakingMeaning: Are you in a 'I need to change my life' moment? Judgement says: this is genuine. Listen, act.
advice: Today write the 'inner voice' you've long ignored. One sentence — what does it say?
affirmation: I respond to the call. My old state is no longer on me.

name: World (Tarot XXI)
essence: The World card is Tarot's final stop. The naked figure dances, four creatures (human, eagle, lion, bull) at four corners. Wholeness complete.
lightAspect: Completion, closing of a cycle, celebrating wholeness. The feeling of 'everything is in its place'.
shadowAspect: Thinking 'I arrived' and freezing. Not seeing the start of the next cycle.
dreamMeaning: A dance in a dream, all four directions at once, the whole world one breath — World. A cycle is closing fully.
wakingMeaning: Has a long process ended? World says: celebrate. Don't rush into a new one. Pause on the threshold.
advice: Today write what you have achieved in the last 6 months. Honour — don't be too humble. Then wait.
affirmation: I am complete — ready for the new cycle, but first I celebrate.

name: Sky Father (Zeus / Jupiter)
essence: Zeus / Jupiter, the sky god, holder of lightning, king of Olympus. The peak of masculine authority, rule and order. Both protective and capricious.
lightAspect: Taking responsibility, governing from a broad perspective, being the roof of family / community. The capacity to decide.
shadowAspect: Patriarchal pride, clinging to the throne, conditional love. The inflation of the male ego.
dreamMeaning: Standing before a king in a dream, lightning striking, a voice from above — Sky Father. An authority speaks inside you — own it or face it.
wakingMeaning: You are in a 'boss' role in your life (work, family, project). Sky Father says: carry the power, don't ego-fy. Authority is service.
advice: Today think of an area where you wield authority. Is there a piece you've ego-fied? Give it back.
affirmation: I can carry authority but I am not my ego. My decisions are in service.

name: Sky Mother (Hera / Frigg)
essence: Hera, the wife of Zeus, guardian of marriage and dignity. Frigg in Norse, the queen who knows the future, wife of Odin. Symbol of mature feminine authority.
lightAspect: Loyalty, dignity, the sacredness of marriage / commitment, feminine maturity.
shadowAspect: Jealousy, revenge, living in the husband's shadow. The toxic form of ownership.
dreamMeaning: Seeing a queen in a dream, wearing a crown, a wedding ceremony — Sky Mother. A 'dignity' matter is inside you.
wakingMeaning: Are you losing respect in a commitment? Sky Mother says: asking for dignity is a right. Not bargain — boundary.
advice: Today name a situation harming your dignity. Not silently but consciously — a message, a conversation, a letter.
affirmation: My dignity is not for negotiation. I speak with my mature feminine voice.

name: Wise Warrior (Athena / Minerva)
essence: Athena was born armed from Zeus's head — the goddess who makes war with strategy and builds wisdom with art and craft. Union of intellect and courage.
lightAspect: Strategy, courage coordinated with mind, love of craft, resolute wisdom.
shadowAspect: Over-control, dismissal of feelings, cold calculation.
dreamMeaning: An owl in a dream, a spear, a shield, a wise warrior woman — Athena. Your strategy and courage have joined.
wakingMeaning: You are about to enter a 'battle' — perhaps not physical but a struggle. Athena says: start with the head. Plan, then move.
advice: Today re-examine an important decision as 'strategic, not emotional'. Not coldness — clarity.
affirmation: My mind and courage work together. I have the right to wise struggle.

name: Wild Maiden (Artemis / Diana)
essence: Artemis, moon goddess, protector of the forest, the unmarried wild maiden. The ancient meaning of 'virgin' — not 'outside of marriage' but 'belonging to herself'.
lightAspect: Independence, bond with nature, being enough for yourself. Being complete without being attached to anyone.
shadowAspect: Disconnection, coldness, refusing intimacy. Staying alone with the line 'I am strong alone'.
dreamMeaning: Alone in the forest in a dream, shooting an arrow, meeting eyes with a deer — Artemis. The 'her own' woman inside wants to speak.
wakingMeaning: Are you losing yourself in relationships? Artemis says: return to yourself first. Bonds flow only from fullness.
advice: Today make a decision without consulting anyone. Go somewhere alone. This is Artemis practice.
affirmation: I belong to myself. Being full before bonding is my right.

name: Lover (Aphrodite / Venus)
essence: Aphrodite, the goddess of love born of sea foam. Not only romantic — aesthetic, attraction, the driving force of creation.
lightAspect: Aesthetic awareness, attraction, capacity for genuine love, creating beauty.
shadowAspect: Manipulative charm, narcissism, using lust for power.
dreamMeaning: Sea foam in a dream, rose, swan, naked feminine beauty — Aphrodite. Love and aesthetics inside are awakening.
wakingMeaning: How do you carry attractiveness in life? Aphrodite says: beauty is an authority — denying it is also a right.
advice: Today be beautiful for yourself — a ritual care, a perfume, an outfit. Aesthetic is spiritual.
affirmation: I have the right to beauty and love. I do not deny my magnetism.

name: Warrior (Ares / Mars)
essence: Ares — the unloved Greek war god. When he becomes Roman Mars he is elevated — brings spring, protects. Together they are the two faces of raw masculine energy.
lightAspect: Courage, fighting capacity, instinct to protect, defending boundaries.
shadowAspect: Aggressive, uncontrolled anger, tendency to physical violence, destruction for destruction's sake.
dreamMeaning: Fighting in a dream, brawling, a bloody scene, holding a sword — Ares. Repressed anger or legitimate defence within.
wakingMeaning: Do you have suppressed anger? Ares says: express — with words, not destruction. Channelled, it becomes energy.
advice: Today release your anger physically — run, punching bag, scream out loud. Then speak.
affirmation: My anger is information. I don't deny it, but I won't be destructive.

name: Creative Craftsman (Hephaestus / Vulcan)
essence: Hephaestus is lame, ugly, but the weapons of the gods come from his hands. Archetype of the wounded craftsman — known not by form but by creation.
lightAspect: Love of making with hands, craft patience, honouring invisible labour. Wound turned into creation.
shadowAspect: Normalising invisibility, devaluing yourself, always making someone else's weapon but forgetting your own.
dreamMeaning: Forging iron in a dream, working at a hearth, using hands — Hephaestus. There is a creation pulse inside; take it in hand.
wakingMeaning: Are you creating but no one sees? Hephaestus says: the value of creation doesn't come from visibility. But this needs remembering.
advice: Today make something with your hands. Perfection not required — the process is the value.
affirmation: Creation is my expression. I create not for being seen, for being.

name: God of Ecstasy (Dionysus / Bacchus)
essence: Dionysus, god of wine, dance, collective ecstasy. While Apollo is control, Dionysus is surrender. The sacred form of ego dissolution.
lightAspect: Ecstasy, dance, artistic wildness, union through ego dissolution. Ritual collective joy.
shadowAspect: Addiction, loss of control, losing oneself by surrendering to lust.
dreamMeaning: At a festival in a dream, in a vineyard, dancing with a group — Dionysus. The desire to 'let go' is inside.
wakingMeaning: A very controlled period? Dionysus says: a moment of release is essential. But consciously — not for burnout.
advice: Today for 10 minutes alone play music loud and move with your body. Without judgement.
affirmation: I have the right to ecstasy and surrender. Not always control — sometimes release.

name: King of the Underworld (Hades / Pluto)
essence: Hades is the god of the underworld — not of death but its administrator. At the same time the owner of hidden riches (jewels, minerals). The king of the unconscious.
lightAspect: Depth, ownership of unconscious riches, mastery in shadow work.
shadowAspect: Closing inward, disconnection from life, depression, not returning to the world.
dreamMeaning: Underground in a dream, in a cave, talking with the dead — Hades. There is a treasure in your unconscious — descend.
wakingMeaning: Is an 'inner work' (therapy, deep journalling, shadow work) approaching? Hades says: descend. The treasure is underground.
advice: Today dump a subject you 'repressed' in the past onto a page. Without judgement. Bring the underground knowledge to daylight.
affirmation: I can descend into darkness. The underworld is the place of wealth.

name: Harvest Mother (Demeter / Ceres)
essence: Demeter governs the seasons; when her daughter Persephone is lost she dries the world. Both abundance and mourning — the two ends of the maternal bond.
lightAspect: Giving motherhood, capacity to nourish, deep harmony with nature, the strength to continue even in loss.
shadowAspect: Excessive mourning, using love to control, not being able to release a (literal or symbolic) child.
dreamMeaning: Fertile fields in a dream, harvest, looking for someone you lost — Demeter. You carry a give-loss cycle.
wakingMeaning: Have you lost or are losing someone (a child, project, loved one)? Demeter says: mourning is proof of giving love. But the seasons will turn.
advice: Today honour a loss — a small candle, a note, a silent moment.
affirmation: I can both give and mourn. Both are motherhood.

name: Destroyer-Transformer (Shiva)
essence: Shiva is the destroyer-transformer of the Hindu trinity. As Nataraja he dances creation's dance, making absence the precondition of creation.
lightAspect: Conscious destruction, courage to let the old go, living in dance. Acceptance of emptiness.
shadowAspect: Constantly burning down without starting the new. Reducing tantra to lust.
dreamMeaning: Dancing figure in a dream, a god inside a ring of fire, a snake at the neck — Shiva. You are in the middle of a transformation.
wakingMeaning: Are you experiencing a conscious 'burning' in life? Shiva says: ash is also part of creation. Wait patiently.
advice: Today write what 'you need to let go of', then ritually burn it safely (in a sink, then water down the drain). Ritual leaves traces.
affirmation: Destruction is the partner of creation. I am open to transformation.

name: Protector (Vishnu)
essence: Vishnu is the god protecting cosmic order. When justice is broken he descends as an 'avatar' — Rama, Krishna, Buddha. The continually returning protector.
lightAspect: Maintaining order, taking the right form in a crisis moment, universal love.
shadowAspect: Owning the status quo, blocking necessary destruction, trying to always 'be good'.
dreamMeaning: A protective figure in a dream, sitting on a lotus, a blue-skinned god — Vishnu. You are in a 'protection' role in some area.
wakingMeaning: Does your circle expect you to 'preserve order'? Vishnu says: the right form at the right moment. You don't have to be the same you always.
advice: Today bring a 'new form' to your life. Hair, clothes, routine — small is fine. Elasticity in form is freedom.
affirmation: I can protect order but my form can change. Flexibility is my strength.

name: Wild Mother (Kali)
essence: Kali wears a necklace of skulls, drips with blood, tongue protruding. Appears frightening but is in fact the destroyer of the ego — the deepest motherhood.
lightAspect: Courage to destroy the ego, wild compassion, swallowing the false. The capacity to 'frighten with love'.
shadowAspect: Destruction for destruction, mixing wildness with violence, shattering what you love.
dreamMeaning: A wild goddess in a dream, skull garland, a blood-stained figure — Kali. An ego inside needs to die — don't fear it.
wakingMeaning: Is a 'false' area of yours collapsing? Kali says: this destruction is not love, it is love's result. Don't resist.
advice: Today think of an ego (role, label, status) you carry. How would life be without it? Just think.
affirmation: Destroying ego is not frightening. The wild mother inside carries love.

name: Threshold Breaker (Ganesha)
essence: Ganesha is the elephant-headed god, both placer and remover of obstacles. Stands at the door of beginnings. Playful in the lap of wisdom.
lightAspect: Looking at obstacles with wisdom, honouring beginnings, uniting play and seriousness.
shadowAspect: Personalising obstacles, constantly waiting for someone to remove them.
dreamMeaning: An elephant in a dream, obstacles on the road, a wise child sitting on your lap — Ganesha. You are at the threshold of a beginning.
wakingMeaning: Did an obstacle appear before a beginning? Ganesha says: this obstacle is a gift. Either you'll remove it or learn from it.
advice: Today before a beginning sit for 5 minutes. Set an intention. The Ganesha practice is simple but deep.
affirmation: Obstacles are gifts. I honour beginnings.

name: Wisdom Sacrifice (Odin)
essence: Odin gave one eye to Mimir's well for wisdom, hung on the world-tree for nine days and learned the runes. Symbol of the price of wisdom.
lightAspect: Sacrifice for wisdom, deep seeing, spiritual discipline. Awareness 'I am paying the cost'.
shadowAspect: Romanticising pain, unnecessary sacrifice, hero complex.
dreamMeaning: A one-eyed man in a dream, hanging, ravens, runes — Odin. You have been willing to give something for knowledge.
wakingMeaning: Did you give up something for learning or awareness? Odin says: the price is real but meaningful. Don't regret.
advice: Today think of knowledge / meaning you sacrificed for. Does it still deserve it? Honour.
affirmation: Wisdom is not free. I honour every price I paid.

name: Thunder (Thor)
essence: Thor protects Asgard with Mjölnir (the hammer). Unlike noble Odin, he is on the side of ordinary people. The folk version of the protective masculine.
lightAspect: Protection, being on the side of ordinary people, honest power, courage.
shadowAspect: Striking without thinking, coarseness, looking for simple solutions.
dreamMeaning: Thunder in a dream, a hammer, fighting — Thor. The protective masculine inside you is active.
wakingMeaning: Are you protecting someone or a group? Thor says: this power is real. But think — there are things that aren't solved by hitting.
advice: Today give silent support to someone you protect. Words not needed.
affirmation: My power is in service. I know to protect but not to destroy.

name: King of Abundance (Frey)
essence: Frey is from the Vanir, god of abundance and peace. He even gave his sword for his love. Archetype of masculine love and giving.
lightAspect: Abundance, sharing, ability to give for love, peace-making.
shadowAspect: Over-giving, losing yourself, offering boundless gifts.
dreamMeaning: Harvest in a dream, giving / receiving a gift, a peace scene — Frey. The desire to give is in you — balance it.
wakingMeaning: Are you giving someone too much? Frey says: giving is beautiful, but don't give your 'sword' — that is your own protection.
advice: Today review what you give. From love or from fear?
affirmation: Giving is fullness. But I also honour my own boundary.

name: Solar King (Ra / Apollo)
essence: Ra is Egypt's sun god, Apollo Greece's. Both are light of consciousness, mind, music, prophecy. The solar king archetype — bringing the day.
lightAspect: Clear consciousness, mind, the power of expression, art and music.
shadowAspect: Excessive rationality, denying the lunar side, getting stuck in the light.
dreamMeaning: Rising sun in a dream, bright light, music playing — Ra/Apollo. A new clarity dawns inside.
wakingMeaning: Do you want to 'be seen', to be on stage in some area? Solar King says: don't hide your light. But don't forget your lunar side.
advice: Today look at sunrise for 2 minutes (or a sunrise photo).
affirmation: I have the right to my inner light. To be seen is not to fear.

name: Death Guide (Anubis)
essence: Anubis is jackal-headed, the guide who takes the dead to the underworld. Weighs the heart against the feather — if heavy the soul dies, if light it continues.
lightAspect: Guiding through endings, threshold protection, being with you in moments of passage.
shadowAspect: Becoming overly preoccupied with the dead, grief complex, not returning to the living.
dreamMeaning: A jackal in a dream, scales, your heart being weighed — Anubis. You are at the threshold of a passage; approach endings consciously.
wakingMeaning: Are you guiding an ending (yours or someone else's)? Anubis says: do it lightly. A heavy heart cannot pass.
advice: Today think of a 'regret' you've long carried. Can you let it go? Just ask.
affirmation: I consciously accompany endings. I am learning to forgive the heavy heart.

name: Scribe-Magician (Thoth / Hermes Trismegistus)
essence: Thoth is ibis-headed, lunar-linked, the god who gives writing, number and magic to humanity. In the Hermetic tradition he merges with the 'thrice-great Hermes'.
lightAspect: Putting knowledge into writing, using symbol, the power of language, keeping records.
shadowAspect: Manipulating with words, hiding knowledge, arrogant learning.
dreamMeaning: A scribe in a dream, an old library, parchment, a bird-headed figure — Thoth. Call to write / carry knowledge.
wakingMeaning: Do you have the urge to write a knowledge / experience? Thoth says: write. Words are magic.
advice: Today journal for 10 minutes. No topic — just flow. Words find their own way.
affirmation: Writing is my magic. Words shape my world.

name: Mother of Magic (Isis)
essence: When her husband Osiris was dismembered, Isis collected the pieces and put them together. Goddess of magic, healing, and re-uniting parts.
lightAspect: Integrating parts, deep motherhood, magical healing. The power of love beyond.
shadowAspect: Using magic for control, trying to 'fix' another.
dreamMeaning: Gathering pieces in a dream, sheltering wing, a healer woman — Isis. There is an integrative power inside you.
wakingMeaning: Are you trying to gather the 'pieces' of someone (yourself even)? Isis says: do it with love, not control.
advice: Today consciously gather a scattered area (a space, project, relationship). Order is a form of love.
affirmation: I gather the pieces with love. The integrative power is inside me.

name: Goddess of Compassion (Tara / Kuan Yin)
essence: Green Tara in Buddhism is the feminine bodhisattva who runs swiftly to help. In Chinese, Kuan Yin — 'she who hears the cries'. The pure form of compassion.
lightAspect: Limitless compassion, capacity to carry another's pain, quick help.
shadowAspect: Overcompassion — losing yourself, trying to save everyone, burnout.
dreamMeaning: A compassionate woman in a dream, sitting on a lotus, someone wiping tears — Tara. Compassion is awakening within.
wakingMeaning: Do your surroundings expect a lot from you? Tara says: compassion seems infinite but your energy is finite. Boundary is also mercy.
advice: Today first create a 'moment of compassion' for yourself. Then for another. Order matters.
affirmation: My compassion is first for me, then for another. I do not burn out.

name: Nature Shaman (Pachamama)
essence: Pachamama is the Earth Mother of the Andean peoples. A ritual relationship with nature — giving, taking, balancing. The forefather of modern ecological consciousness.
lightAspect: Ritual relationship with nature, take-give balance, ecological awareness.
shadowAspect: Nature romance — speaking of 'natural living' without actually bonding.
dreamMeaning: Touching the earth in a dream, walking in a garden, the change of season — Pachamama call. Time to reconnect with nature.
wakingMeaning: Are you cramped in the city? Pachamama says: a piece of nature is essential. Touch the earth — literally.
advice: Today walk for 5 minutes barefoot on earth. If not possible, touch a plant.
affirmation: My bond with the earth is sacred. I am not separate from nature.

name: Fox-Trickster (Loki)
essence: Loki is the Norse pantheon's trickster — shape-shifter, rule-breaker, both helper and betrayer. The chaos that rattles the status quo.
lightAspect: Rule-breaking wit, unexpected solutions, shaking up status quo.
shadowAspect: Betrayal, not standing behind your word, chaos for chaos.
dreamMeaning: A shapeshifter in a dream, being deceived, an unexpected trick — Loki. Stillness is breaking in some area.
wakingMeaning: Is your life too tidy? Loki says: a bit of chaos is essential. Or the reverse — if someone is deceiving you, see it.
